title: kernel commit bfb819ea20ce8bbeeba17e1a6418bf8bda91fc28 breaks podman
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
I found that podman couldn't starting my container after upgrade kernel to 5.13-rc4
This is strace output:
```
newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/mikhail/.config/containers/storage.conf", 0xc00055b7c8, 0) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
futex(0xc0000b2d50, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
rt_sigprocmask(SIG_SETMASK, ~[HUP INT QUIT ILL TRAP ABRT BUS FPE SEGV TERM STKFLT CHLD PROF SYS RTMIN RT_1 RT_2], NULL, 8) = 0
futex(0xc0000b2d50, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
rt_sigprocmask(SIG_SETMASK, ~[HUP INT QUIT ILL TRAP ABRT BUS FPE SEGV TERM STKFLT CHLD PROF SYS RTMIN RT_1 RT_2], NULL, 8) = 0
futex(0xc0000b2d50, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0xc000181550, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x560592762c70, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL) = -1 EAGAIN (Resource temporarily unavailable)
rt_sigprocmask(SIG_SETMASK, ~[HUP INT QUIT ILL TRAP ABRT BUS FPE SEGV TERM STKFLT CHLD PROF SYS RTMIN RT_1 RT_2], NULL, 8) = 0
futex(0xc000181550, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0xc0001e2150, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x560592762c70, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULLError: reset `/proc/self/attr/keycreate`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
) = ?
+++ exited with 126 +++
```

And I already bisected kernel commit.
```
[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect start
[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect bad v5.13-rc4
[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good ad9f25d33860
Bisecting: 264 revisions left to test after this (roughly 8 steps)
[8508b97ae2b6c73c2c09798c9fa9d27ec57ff1dc] Merge tag 'clang-features-v5.13-rc4' of git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/kees/linux

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect bad
Bisecting: 112 revisions left to test after this (roughly 7 steps)
[5e7923acbd86d0ff29269688d8a9c47ad091dd46] net/mlx5e: Fix error path of updating netdev queues

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 56 revisions left to test after this (roughly 6 steps)
[3ed0a585bfadb6bd7080f11184adbc9edcce7dbc] mptcp: avoid error message on infinite mapping

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 27 revisions left to test after this (roughly 5 steps)
[d7c5303fbc8ac874ae3e597a5a0d3707dc0230b4] Merge tag 'net-5.13-rc4' of git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/netdev/net

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect bad
Bisecting: 14 revisions left to test after this (roughly 4 steps)
[1bad6fd52be4ce12d207e2820ceb0f29ab31fc53] bpf, selftests: Adjust few selftest result_unpriv outcomes

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 7 revisions left to test after this (roughly 3 steps)
[3d227a0b0ce319edbff6fd0d8af4d66689e477cc] mtd: rawnand: txx9ndfmc: Fix external use of SW Hamming ECC helper

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 3 revisions left to test after this (roughly 2 steps)
[62f3415db237b8d2aa9a804ff84ce2efa87df179] net: phy: Document phydev::dev_flags bits allocation

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 2 revisions left to test after this (roughly 1 step)
[562b4e91d3b221f737f84ff78ee7d348c8a6891f] mtd: parsers: ofpart: fix parsing subpartitions

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 0 revisions left to test after this (roughly 1 step)
[7ac3a1c1ae5152e1d18cf6df5b6c3c9847535e78] Merge tag 'mtd/fixes-for-5.13-rc4' of git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/mtd/linux

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect bad
Bisecting: 0 revisions left to test after this (roughly 0 steps)
[bfb819ea20ce8bbeeba17e1a6418bf8bda91fc28] proc: Check /proc/$pid/attr/ writes against file opener

[mikhail@fedora linux]$ git bisect bad
bfb819ea20ce8bbeeba17e1a6418bf8bda91fc28 is the first bad commit
commit bfb819ea20ce8bbeeba17e1a6418bf8bda91fc28
Author: Kees Cook <keescook@chromium.org>
Date:   Tue May 25 12:37:35 2021 -0700

    proc: Check /proc/$pid/attr/ writes against file opener
    
    Fix another "confused deputy" weakness[1]. Writes to /proc/$pid/attr/
    files need to check the opener credentials, since these fds do not
    transition state across execve(). Without this, it is possible to
    trick another process (which may have different credentials) to write
    to its own /proc/$pid/attr/ files, leading to unexpected and possibly
    exploitable behaviors.
    
    [1] https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/latest/security/credentials.html?highlight=confused#open-file-credentials
    
    Fixes: 1da177e4c3f41 ("Linux-2.6.12-rc2")
    Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
    Signed-off-by: Kees Cook <keescook@chromium.org>
    Signed-off-by: Linus Torvalds <torvalds@linux-foundation.org>

 fs/proc/base.c | 4 ++++
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+)
```

comments.body:
I am getting this same error message in Fedora, using packages from `updates-testing`

```shell
$ rpm -qa | grep -e podman -e container | sort
containernetworking-plugins-1.0.0-0.2.rc1.fc34.x86_64
containers-common-1-16.fc34.noarch
container-selinux-2.162.2-1.fc34.noarch
podman-3.2.0-0.2.rc3.fc34.x86_64
podman-plugins-3.2.0-3.fc34.x86_64
systemd-container-248.3-1.fc34.x86_64
```

```shell
$ podman --version
podman version 3.2.0-rc3
```

```shell
$ uname -srv
Linux 5.12.9-300.fc34.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Jun 3 13:51:40 UTC 2021
```

```shell
$ podman run --rm fedora:34
Error: reset `/proc/self/attr/keycreate`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
```

I haven't tried booting into older kernel versions yet, I will see if a previous version works.


Update: Podman does work on the previous kernel package 5.12.8-300

```shell
$ uname -srv
Linux 5.12.8-300.fc34.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri May 28 15:20:54 UTC 2021
$ podman run --interactive --tty --rm fedora
[root@f0640e56a991 /]# exit
exit
$
```
I believe @giuseppe is looking at this as it's coming out of crun?
yes, this is fixed with crun 0.20.1 that was released yesterday: https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/687
Confirmed this is fixed on Fedora 34 with new packages in `updates-testing`
This is still broken for me in today's rawhide.  The reason why is documented in the linked Fedora image issue.

Looks like on kernel side this issue was fixed with commit [591a22c14d3f45cc38bd1931c593c221df2f1881](https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/commit/?h=v5.13-rc5&id=591a22c14d3f45cc38bd1931c593c221df2f1881) even with old podman.
```
$ rpm -qa | grep -e podman -e container | sort
containernetworking-plugins-1.0.0-11.1.git8de0287.fc35.x86_64
containers-common-1-19.fc35.noarch
container-selinux-2.162.2-2.dev.git61b862a.fc35.noarch
podman-3.1.0-0.42.dev.gitd999328.fc35.x86_64
podman-plugins-3.1.0-0.42.dev.gitd999328.fc35.x86_64
systemd-container-248.3-1.fc35.x86_64
```
yeah, the problem is that, because of the crun version numbering problem, upgrading users aren't getting the new crun.

Confirmed that this is now fixed in current rawhide:

```
Downgraded:
  crun 0.20.3-0.12.git8d6a8b5.fc35 -> 0.20.1.1-0.13.git7adf4d0.fc35
```
```
[jberkus@fedora ~]$ podman start --attach ff2
WARN: Jupyter Notebook deprecation notice https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks#jupyter-notebook-deprecation-notice.
Executing the command: jupyter notebook
```

There's a new issue, though, so I'll open a new bug for that.
